rulings.Case Title: CATHERINE HERRERA WATSON VS. TONY GARNICKI
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice & Motion For Vexatious-Litigant Prefiling Order
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 17, 2021 line 1. DEFENDANT CHARLES MA'S Notice & Motion For Vexatious-Litigant Prefiling Order is GRANTED. Plaintiff in this matter continually files motions that lack minimal substantive and procedural merit. Even upon being provided specific instructions by the Court as to the proper way to proceed (e.g. requirements for motion for leave to amend; requirements for obtaining continuances; requirements regarding proper communication with the Court and parties), Plaintiff continues to place motions on calendar. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TONY GARNICKI ET AL VS. ANCHOR LOANS LP, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-20-584439, CGC-20-584440, CUD-20-667549, CUD-20-667550, CUD-20-667551 WITH CASE CGC-20-584236
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 17, 2021 line 2. PLAINTIFFS TONY GARNICKI, OCEAN AVENUE REAL ESTATE FUND LLC, AND SANCHEZ RE, LLC'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-20-584439, CGC-20-584440, CUD-20-667549, CUD-20-667550, CUD-20-667551 WITH CASE CGC-20-584236 is continued to June 4, 2021. By no later than May 21, 2021 Plaintiff in action 584236 to file a proof of service for Summons and First Amended Complaint. No later than May 21, 2021 defendants in all unlawful detainer action to file declarations stating whether the properties are used by the defendants as their primary residence or primary place of business (and the nature of the business); and whether the properties are occupied by third parties, e.g. tenants licensees. By no later than May 21, 2021. Plaintiffs in all UD actions to file declarations specifying in detail the carrying costs associated with the properties, e.g. insurance, mortgage, utilities, etc. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: BUSH STREET APARTMENT GROUP, LLC. VS. BALMORAL RESIDENCES LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside The Defaults Of Yale Residences And Tony Brettkelly
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 17, 2021 line 3. DEFENDANT YALE RESIDENCES, LLC, TONY BRETTKELLY Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside The Defaults Of Yale Residences And Tony Brettkelly is continued to May 21, 2021. No later than May 17, 2021 Plaintiff to submit supplemental briefing limited to citation to cases standing for the proposition that Summons and Complaint may be served per the parties' agreement regarding service of "notices" required on permitted under the Lease and/or that Summons and Complaint are encompassed within the definition of "notice" under the lease. No further argument is permitted. Supplemental brief shall be limited to citation to cases or code sections. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
